body.body:
NINTH CRYSTALLIZATION: Templates are the language primitives of the matrix layer. Every design pattern currently in Python gets expressed as a parameterized template Memory node. Templates expand into habits at seed time (no runtime machinery). The Python reader is the first target — re-express as a complete habit program. The meta-level: map the extraction process itself as habits so Igor can do it without CC. End state: Igor's behavior is expressed in matrix language; Python is bootstrap scaffolding that comes down. 

Phase 1: enumerate ~10-15 core patterns (WONDER, FILE_ITERATOR, THRESHOLD_ALERT, SCHEDULER_TICK, READER_LOOP, MEMORY_DEPOSIT, SEARCH_AND_RESPOND, CONDITION_GATE, ERROR_RECOVERY, ASYNC_DELEGATE). 
Phase 2: express each as TEMPLATE Memory node with parameter slots. 
Phase 3: re-express Python reader as instantiated templates — first working habit program. 
Phase 4: map extraction process as habits — Igor can recognize patterns and seed templates himself. 
Process: Claude+Igor enumerate patterns together. Ask Igor what we missed. Igor names the language (we invented one by accident). 
NOTE: Supersedes T-yield-primitive (call stack is non-biological; chained reactive habits IS the yield mechanism).

BROKEN INTO: T-template-schema → T-template-seed-patterns → T-reader-as-habit-program → T-template-extractor-habit → T-language-spec

LANGUAGE NAME: Engram (proposed by Igor, 2026-03-22). From Semon 1904.
body.title: Templates as matrix language primitives — extract all Igor design patterns, re-express reader as first habit program
body.result: Epic complete: T-template-schema (D209, tpl-wonder) + T-template-seed-patterns (this sprint) together deliver all 21 Engram patterns as TEMPLATE nodes in the matrix. template_tools.py: instantiate/list/validate tools. Remaining: T-reader-as-habit-program, T-template-extractor-habit, T-language-spec.
